title: 1994 Upper Yamuna Water Agreement: All you need to know about the MoU signed by Haryana and Rajasthan
content:
• Rajasthan and Haryana signed an agreement on Monday for the implementation of the Yamuna Water Project, resolving an issue that had been pending for nearly three decades.
• Under the new agreement, approximately 580 Million Cubic Meters (MCM) of water will be supplied from the Yamuna Canal to Rajasthan through three underground pipelines from July to October.
• The original Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) on the sharing of Upper Yamuna Basin waters was signed on May 12, 1994, by Uttar Pradesh, Haryana, Rajasthan, Himachal Pradesh, and the National Capital Territory of Delhi, with Uttarakhand included after its creation in 2000.

title: US-Iran war news LIVE: US Special Envoy Steve Witkoff heads to Qatar, says report
content:
• US Special Envoy Steve Witkoff and Senior Adviser Jared Kushner are scheduled to travel to Doha, Qatar, on Tuesday for discussions with Iranian officials.
• Iran's delegation is visiting Qatar to oversee the implementation of the 14-point Memorandum of Understanding (MoU), particularly Article 11, which requires the US to ensure Iran has full access to its frozen/restricted funds.
• Pakistan LNG Ltd. purchased a liquefied natural gas (LNG) cargo from BP Plc on Monday for delivery between June 30 and July 4 at $16.74 per million British thermal units (mmBtu), which was higher than the mid-$15 per mmBtu Asian spot LNG price.

title: US Supreme Court rulings on birthright citizenship, transgender athletes' rights, election campaign finance today
content:
• The US Supreme Court concluded its 2025-26 term on Tuesday, June 30, by handing down final decisions on politically charged legal battles.
• One major case concerns federal limits on coordinated campaign spending between political parties, such as the Republican National Committee (RNC) and Democratic National Committee (DNC), with a decision potentially reshaping campaign fundraising for the 2026 midterm elections.
• The Court also ruled on challenges to President Donald Trump's executive order seeking to end automatic US citizenship for children born to undocumented immigrants, which is being contested under the Citizenship Clause of the 14th Amendment.

title: Deepak Parekh bats for further consolidation in PSU banks, FDI cap hike to boost GDP growth
content:
• The number of India's state-owned banks shrunk from 27 in 2017 to 12 by 2020, following the amalgamation of 10 banks into 4 entities.
• Discussions are ongoing between the finance ministry and the Reserve Bank of India to raise the foreign direct investment (FDI) cap for public sector banks to 49% from the current 20%.
• Indian banks' gross non-performing assets (NPAs) are currently below 2%, which is the lowest level recorded in decades.

title: US Supreme Court delivers blow to Trump over mail-in ballots
content:
• On Monday, June 29, the US Supreme Court, in a 5-4 decision, upheld state laws allowing certain mail-in ballots received after Election Day, rejecting a Republican-backed challenge to Mississippi's five-day grace period.
• Justice Amy Coney Barrett authored the majority opinion for the US Supreme Court's June 29 ruling, joined by Chief Justice John Roberts and three liberal justices, while Justices Samuel Alito, Clarence Thomas, Neil Gorsuch, and Brett Kavanaugh dissented.
• The US Supreme Court's June 29 decision overturned a 2024 ruling by the 5th US Circuit Court of Appeals, which had previously mandated that federal election laws require ballots to be both cast and received on Election Day.

title: 'It's in last 1 or 2%': US envoy Sergio Gor declares India-US trade pact nears finish line
content:
• The India-US bilateral trade agreement is on the verge of completion, with US Ambassador to India Sergio Gor announcing that only "1 or 2 per cent" of the legal text remains to be finalized.
• The US Mission in India, led by Ambassador Sergio Gor, facilitated a massive $20.5 billion in brand-new investments flowing back into the United States this year alone.
• India and the US are focused on reaching a USD 500 billion bilateral trade target, originally championed by President Donald Trump and Prime Minister Narendra Modi.

title: Draft National Electricity Data Sharing Framework unveiled
content:
• The Power Ministry unveiled a draft National Electricity Data Sharing Framework.
• The last date for stakeholders to submit views on the Draft National Electricity Data Sharing Framework is July 21.
• The framework proposes the establishment of a National Electricity Data Centre and a National Electricity Data Portal.

title: NPCI turns to AI to stop frauds from siphoning off money
content:
• NPCI is implementing a new AI-based system to combat digital payment fraud across India's entire payments network.
• The AI initiative involves a pilot program with nearly a dozen shortlisted banks, where each bank will run an NPCI-developed AI model locally on their transaction data.
• This AI system, built on a federated learning architecture, compresses the fraud detection window from hours or days to mere seconds, enabling real-time alerts or freezes to intercept funds before they are fully dispersed.

title: Haryana, Rajasthan sign pact to implement Yamuna water project
content:
• Haryana and Rajasthan signed a Memorandum of Agreement (MoA) to implement the Yamuna water project, formalized under the chairmanship of Union Home Minister Shah.
• An agreement on the sharing of Yamuna waters among the Upper Yamuna Basin states, reached in 1994, remained unimplemented for over three decades until its recent formalization.
• Under the Yamuna water project, Rajasthan's water share will be supplied from the Hathnikund Barrage via an underground pipeline through the Western Yamuna Canal system during the monsoon season.

title: Tomato, onion stoke fears of higher food inflation
content:
• Tomato prices have increased by 25% on a year-on-year basis.
• Onion prices have risen by 3.3% on a year-on-year basis.
• Potato prices are 17% lower on a year-on-year basis.
